scores.correctness_notes: Core change/task model is correct (id, status, ready, err, timestamps, state persistence). However, the gold standard covers 6 approaches (CLI pebble changes/tasks, REST API polling, REST API blocking wait, REST API filtering, Notices/change-update) while the agent covers only the Python/ops client side. Key CLI and REST API details are entirely absent.
scores.hallucination_notes: container.replan(timeout=30.0) and container.restart('myservice', timeout=30.0) — the ops Container.replan() and restart() methods do not accept a timeout parameter in the standard API. The timeout/delay parameters belong to the lower-level pebble client methods, not the Container abstraction. Also, container.replan() returning a ChangeID is questionable — the ops Container.replan() typically returns None. Minor inaccuracies rather than wholesale fabrications.
